articles.author: Lorian Belanger, Brian P. D. Hannon
articles.description: The Trump administration offered a 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran, a person briefed on the contours of the proposal said late Tuesday, even as the U.S. military prepared to call up at least 1,000 more troops to supplement some 50,000 troops already in the Mideast.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump administration offers 15-point ceasefire plan to Iran

articles.author: Linley Sanders, Michael Catalini
articles.description: A new AP-NORC poll finds most Americans believe recent military action against Iran has gone too far and many are worried about affording gasoline. As the war launched by the U.S. and Israel continues in its fourth week, the survey indicates that while President Donald Trump’s approval rating is holding steady, the conflict could be turning into a major political liability for his Republican administration. About 59% of Americans say U.S. military action has been excessive. Meanwhile, 45% are “extremely” or “very” concerned about affording gas in the next few months, up from 30% shortly before Trump took office. However, there's significant support for Trump’s objective of preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon.
articles.title: Most say US military action against Iran has gone too far: poll

articles.author: Konstantin Toropin, Aamer Madhani
articles.description: The U.S. military is preparing to deploy at least 1,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East in the coming days. That's according to three people with knowledge of the plans who spoke on condition of anonymity Tuesday. The unit is considered the Army’s emergency response force and can typically be deployed on short notice. The soldiers are trained to parachute into hostile or contested territory to secure key territory and airfields. The force would include a battalion of the 1st Brigade Combat Team, the division’s commander and staff. It’s the latest addition of American troops after U.S. officials recently said thousands of Marines aboard several Navy ships will be heading to the region.
articles.title: US military sending 1,000 troops from 82nd Airborne to Mideast

articles.author: Kristen Gelineau
articles.description: Humanitarian organizations under intense strain because of the United States’ steep cuts to foreign aid say they are scrambling to find the funds needed to respond to the war in the Middle East. Last year, U.S. President Donald Trump dissolved the U.S. Agency for International Development, which had been the world’s leading donor of humanitarian assistance. That decision forced aid groups around the world to fire thousands of staffers and shutter lifesaving programs. Now, some of those same groups are struggling to mount a response in the Middle East. Already, the United Nations’ refugee agency, UNHCR, estimates 3.2 million people inside Iran and a million people in Lebanon have been displaced since the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran on Feb. 28.
articles.title: Middle East war strains humanitarian groups after US aid reduction

articles.description: Democrat Emily Gregory has won a special election for a Florida state House seat, flipping a district that is home to President Donald Trump’s estate, Mar-a-Lago. The Republican president endorsed Gregory’s rival, Jon Maples. Republican Mike Caruso, who resigned from the seat to become Palm Beach County’s clerk, won the district by 19 percentage points in 2024. Trump won the district by about 10 percentage points in the 2024 presidential election. Democrats are celebrating the victory as the latest sign voters are turning against Trump and the GOP ahead of the midterm elections in November.
articles.title: Democrat Emily Gregory wins Florida race to represent Mar a Lago

articles.author: Gary Robertson
articles.description: North Carolina Senate leader Phil Berger has conceded the Republican primary for his legislative seat. It’s a move that will shake up power in state government. Unofficial tallies showed Berger trailing Rockingham County Sheriff Sam Page Berger by a narrow margin. But Berger conceded on Tuesday. President Donald Trump had endorsed Berger for the seat. Berger has been Senate leader since 2011 and the top architect of the state’s conservative shift. He can remain a senator and Senate leader through year’s end if he loses.
articles.title: Phil Berger concedes North Carolina legislative primary

articles.author: Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Sal Christ
articles.description: As travel disruptions deepen and senators race to end the Homeland Security shutdown, House lawmakers are holding a hearing at 10 a.m. ET to discuss the impacts of the funding lapse.
articles.title: Live updates: DHS officials to give update to Congress as travel delays worsen

articles.author: Eric Tucker
articles.description: A top House Democrat says President Donald Trump showed off a classified map on a 2022 flight to his New Jersey golf club and held onto a record from his first term that was so sensitive only six people would have had access to it. A letter being released Wednesday from Rep. Jamie Raskin of Maryland adds to the public understanding of the long-concluded investigation into Trump’s retention of classified documents at the Republican's Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Florida. Raskin is the top Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee. Raskin's letter quotes from a newly disclosed Justice Department memo from January 2023. The White House says Raskin has “zero credibility.”
articles.title: Trump showed classified map on 2022 plane trip, lawmaker says

articles.author: David Bauder
articles.description: Savannah Guthrie says in a tearful interview on NBC that "'somebody needs to do the right thing” and provide information that leads to a resolution of the case of her missing 84-year-old mother. Guthrie says she wakes up every night wondering about the terror that her mother went through. The “Today” show aired a portion of Hoda Kotb’s interview with her colleague Wednesday and says it will air more on Thursday and Friday — the first interview Guthrie has given since her mother went missing nearly two months ago. Investigators believe Nancy Guthrie was kidnapped or otherwise taken against her will.
articles.title: Savannah Guthrie appeals for help finding her missing mother

articles.author: Scott Bauer
articles.description: A jury in Wisconsin has found a man guilty of election fraud and identity theft for requesting the ballots of Republican state Assembly Speaker Robin Vos and Democratic Racine Mayor Cory Mason without their consent. A Racine County jury on Tuesday found Harry Wait guilty of two misdemeanor election fraud charges and one felony identity theft charge following a two-day trial. He was acquitted of a second count of identity theft. Wait leads a group that makes false election claims. He admitted in 2022 that he requested the ballots to try to prove that the state’s voter registration system is vulnerable to fraud.
articles.title: Wisconsin man convicted of fraud for ordering others' ballots
